sections.paragraph:
ἐμφύρω pf. pass. ptc. ἐμπεφυρμένος (φύρω ‘mix’, s. φύραμα; Aeschyl., Fgm. 446 Mette [=38 N.] ἵπποι δʼ ἐφʼ ἵπποις ἐμπεφυρμένοι in descr. of a confused battle scene; Lycophron 1380; Ezk 22:6 v.l.) to put together/combine in a mass, mix up, knead in ἐμπεφυρμένος τινί mixed up w. someth.=involved (in earthly pursuits) Hm 10, 1, 4; Hs 8, 8, 1; 9, 20, 1f. W. the world m 12, 1, 2. DELG s.v. φύρω.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  2. = Nestle/Aland, Novum Testamentum Graece, 27th ed. Other editions are indicated by a superscript—List 1, beg.
  3. = neuter; note (in bibliographical references)

descr. descr. = description (of inventoried papyrus)

Lycophron Lycophron , trag., IV–III b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

someth. someth. = something

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

m m = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 326
